1	"""
2	Cross-layer contract parsers.
3	
4	Extracts interface contracts (opcodes, bit widths, reset defaults, packet
5	layouts) directly from the source files of each layer:
6	  - Python GUI:  radar_protocol.py
7	  - FPGA RTL:    radar_system_top.v, usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v,
8	                 usb_data_interface.v
9	  - STM32 MCU:   RadarSettings.cpp, main.cpp
10	
11	These parsers do NOT define the expected values — they discover what each
12	layer actually implements, so the test can compare layers against ground
13	truth and find bugs where both sides are wrong (like the 0x06 phantom
14	opcode or the status_words[0] 37-bit truncation).
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	import re
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	# Repository layout (relative to repo root)
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
27	GUI_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "9_Firmware" / "9_3_GUI"
28	FPGA_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "9_Firmware" / "9_2_FPGA"
29	MCU_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "9_Firmware" / "9_1_Microcontroller"
30	MCU_LIB_DIR = MCU_DIR / "9_1_1_C_Cpp_Libraries"
31	MCU_CODE_DIR = MCU_DIR / "9_1_3_C_Cpp_Code"
32	XDC_DIR = FPGA_DIR / "constraints"
33	
34	
35	# ===================================================================
36	# Data structures
37	# ===================================================================
38	
39	@dataclass
40	class OpcodeEntry:
41	    """One opcode as declared in a single layer."""
42	    name: str
43	    value: int
44	    register: str = ""      # Verilog register name it writes to
45	    bit_slice: str = ""     # e.g. "[3:0]", "[15:0]", "[0]"
46	    bit_width: int = 0      # derived from bit_slice
47	    reset_default: int | None = None
48	    is_pulse: bool = False  # True for trigger/request opcodes
49	
50	
51	@dataclass
52	class StatusWordField:
53	    """One field inside a status_words[] entry."""
54	    name: str
55	    word_index: int
56	    msb: int   # bit position in the 32-bit word (0-indexed from LSB)
57	    lsb: int
58	    width: int
59	
60	
61	@dataclass
62	class DataPacketField:
63	    """One field in the 11-byte data packet."""
64	    name: str
65	    byte_start: int   # first byte index (0 = header)
66	    byte_end: int     # last byte index (inclusive)
67	    width_bits: int
68	
69	
70	@dataclass
71	class PacketConstants:
72	    """Header/footer/size constants for a packet type."""
73	    header: int
74	    footer: int
75	    size: int
76	
77	
78	@dataclass
79	class SettingsField:
80	    """One field in the STM32 SET...END settings packet."""
81	    name: str
82	    offset: int        # byte offset from start of payload (after "SET")
83	    size: int          # bytes
84	    c_type: str        # "double" or "uint32_t"
85	
86	
87	@dataclass
88	class GpioPin:
89	    """A GPIO pin with direction."""
90	    name: str
91	    pin_id: str        # e.g. "PD8", "H11"
92	    direction: str     # "output" or "input"
93	    layer: str         # "stm32" or "fpga"
94	
95	
96	@dataclass
97	class ConcatWidth:
98	    """Result of counting bits in a Verilog concatenation."""
99	    total_bits: int
100	    target_bits: int   # width of the register being assigned to
101	    fragments: list[tuple[str, int]] = field(default_factory=list)
102	    truncated: bool = False
103	
104	
105	# ===================================================================
106	# Python layer parser
107	# ===================================================================
108	
109	def parse_python_opcodes(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[int, OpcodeEntry]:
110	    """Parse the Opcode enum from radar_protocol.py.
111	    Returns {opcode_value: OpcodeEntry}.
112	    """
113	    if filepath is None:
114	        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
115	    text = filepath.read_text()
116	
117	    # Find the Opcode class body
118	    match = re.search(r'class Opcode\b.*?(?=\nclass |\Z)', text, re.DOTALL)
119	    if not match:
120	        raise ValueError(f"Could not find 'class Opcode' in {filepath}")
121	
122	    opcodes: dict[int, OpcodeEntry] = {}
123	    for m in re.finditer(r'(\w+)\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)', match.group()):
124	        name = m.group(1)
125	        value = int(m.group(2), 16)
126	        opcodes[value] = OpcodeEntry(name=name, value=value)
127	    return opcodes
128	
129	
130	def parse_python_packet_constants(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, PacketConstants]:
131	    """Extract HEADER_BYTE, FOOTER_BYTE, STATUS_HEADER_BYTE, packet sizes."""
132	    if filepath is None:
133	        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
134	    text = filepath.read_text()
135	
136	    def _find(pattern: str) -> int:
137	        m = re.search(pattern, text)
138	        if not m:
139	            raise ValueError(f"Pattern not found: {pattern}")
140	        val = m.group(1)
141	        return int(val, 16) if val.startswith("0x") else int(val)
142	
143	    header = _find(r'HEADER_BYTE\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)')
144	    footer = _find(r'FOOTER_BYTE\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)')
145	    status_header = _find(r'STATUS_HEADER_BYTE\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)')
146	    data_size = _find(r'DATA_PACKET_SIZE\s*=\s*(\d+)')
147	    status_size = _find(r'STATUS_PACKET_SIZE\s*=\s*(\d+)')
148	
149	    return {
150	        "data": PacketConstants(header=header, footer=footer, size=data_size),
151	        "status": PacketConstants(header=status_header, footer=footer, size=status_size),
152	    }
153	
154	
155	def parse_python_data_packet_fields(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[DataPacketField]:
156	    """
157	    Extract byte offsets from parse_data_packet() by finding struct.unpack_from calls.
158	    Returns fields in byte order.
159	    """
160	    if filepath is None:
161	        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
162	    text = filepath.read_text()
163	
164	    # Find parse_data_packet method body
165	    match = re.search(
166	        r'def parse_data_packet\(.*?\).*?(?=\n    @|\n    def |\nclass |\Z)',
167	        text, re.DOTALL
168	    )
169	    if not match:
170	        raise ValueError("Could not find parse_data_packet()")
171	
172	    body = match.group()
173	    fields: list[DataPacketField] = []
174	
175	    # Match patterns like: range_q = _to_signed16(struct.unpack_from(">H", raw, 1)[0])
176	    for m in re.finditer(
177	        r'(\w+)\s*=\s*_to_signed16\(struct\.unpack_from\("(>[HIBhib])", raw, (\d+)\)',
178	        body
179	    ):
180	        name = m.group(1)
181	        fmt = m.group(2)
182	        offset = int(m.group(3))
183	        fmt_char = fmt[-1].upper()
184	        size = {"H": 2, "I": 4, "B": 1}[fmt_char]
185	        fields.append(DataPacketField(
186	            name=name, byte_start=offset,
187	            byte_end=offset + size - 1,
188	            width_bits=size * 8
189	        ))
190	
191	    # Match detection = raw[9] & 0x01  (direct access)
192	    for m in re.finditer(r'(\w+)\s*=\s*raw\[(\d+)\]\s*&\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)', body):
193	        name = m.group(1)
194	        offset = int(m.group(2))
195	        fields.append(DataPacketField(
196	            name=name, byte_start=offset, byte_end=offset, width_bits=1
197	        ))
198	
199	    # Match intermediate variable pattern: var = raw[N], then field = var & MASK
200	    for m in re.finditer(r'(\w+)\s*=\s*raw\[(\d+)\]', body):
201	        var_name = m.group(1)
202	        offset = int(m.group(2))
203	        # Find fields derived from this intermediate variable
204	        for m2 in re.finditer(
205	            rf'(\w+)\s*=\s*(?:\({var_name}\s*>>\s*\d+\)\s*&|{var_name}\s*&)\s*'
206	            r'(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)',
207	            body,
208	        ):
209	            name = m2.group(1)
210	            # Skip if already captured by direct raw[] access pattern
211	            if not any(f.name == name for f in fields):
212	                fields.append(DataPacketField(
213	                    name=name, byte_start=offset, byte_end=offset,
214	                    width_bits=1
215	                ))
216	
217	    fields.sort(key=lambda f: f.byte_start)
218	    return fields
219	
220	
221	def parse_python_status_fields(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[StatusWordField]:
222	    """
223	    Extract bit shift/mask operations from parse_status_packet().
224	    Returns the fields with word index and bit positions as Python sees them.
225	    """
226	    if filepath is None:
227	        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
228	    text = filepath.read_text()
229	
230	    match = re.search(
231	        r'def parse_status_packet\(.*?\).*?(?=\n    @|\n    def |\nclass |\Z)',
232	        text, re.DOTALL
233	    )
234	    if not match:
235	        raise ValueError("Could not find parse_status_packet()")
236	
237	    body = match.group()
238	    fields: list[StatusWordField] = []
239	
240	    # Pattern: sr.field = (words[N] >> S) & MASK  # noqa: ERA001
241	    for m in re.finditer(
242	        r'sr\.(\w+)\s*=\s*\(words\[(\d+)\]\s*>>\s*(\d+)\)\s*&\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)',
243	        body
244	    ):
245	        name = m.group(1)
246	        word_idx = int(m.group(2))
247	        shift = int(m.group(3))
248	        mask_str = m.group(4)
249	        mask = int(mask_str, 16) if mask_str.startswith("0x") else int(mask_str)
250	        width = mask.bit_length()
251	        fields.append(StatusWordField(
252	            name=name, word_index=word_idx,
253	            msb=shift + width - 1, lsb=shift, width=width
254	        ))
255	
256	    # Pattern: sr.field = words[N] & MASK  (no shift)
257	    for m in re.finditer(
258	        r'sr\.(\w+)\s*=\s*words\[(\d+)\]\s*&\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)',
259	        body
260	    ):
261	        name = m.group(1)
262	        word_idx = int(m.group(2))
263	        mask_str = m.group(3)
264	        mask = int(mask_str, 16) if mask_str.startswith("0x") else int(mask_str)
265	        width = mask.bit_length()
266	        # Skip if already captured by the shift pattern
267	        if not any(f.name == name and f.word_index == word_idx for f in fields):
268	            fields.append(StatusWordField(
269	                name=name, word_index=word_idx,
270	                msb=width - 1, lsb=0, width=width
271	            ))
272	
273	    return fields
274	
275	
276	# ===================================================================
277	# Verilog layer parser
278	# ===================================================================
279	
280	def _parse_bit_slice(s: str) -> int:
281	    """Parse '[15:0]' -> 16, '[0]' -> 1, '' -> 16 (full cmd_value)."""
282	    m = re.match(r'\[(\d+):(\d+)\]', s)
283	    if m:
284	        return int(m.group(1)) - int(m.group(2)) + 1
285	    m = re.match(r'\[(\d+)\]', s)
286	    if m:
287	        return 1
288	    return 16  # default: full 16-bit cmd_value
289	
290	
291	def parse_verilog_opcodes(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[int, OpcodeEntry]:
292	    """
293	    Parse the opcode case statement from radar_system_top.v.
294	    Returns {opcode_value: OpcodeEntry}.
295	    """
296	    if filepath is None:
297	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v"
298	    text = filepath.read_text()
299	
300	    # Find the command decode case block
301	    # Pattern: case statement with 8'hXX opcodes
302	    opcodes: dict[int, OpcodeEntry] = {}
303	
304	    # Pattern 1: Simple assignment — 8'hXX: register <= rhs;
305	    for m in re.finditer(
306	        r"8'h([0-9a-fA-F]{2})\s*:\s*(\w+)\s*<=\s*(.*?)(?:;|$)",
307	        text, re.MULTILINE
308	    ):
309	        value = int(m.group(1), 16)
310	        register = m.group(2)
311	        rhs = m.group(3).strip()
312	
313	        # Determine if it's a pulse (assigned literal 1)
314	        is_pulse = rhs in ("1", "1'b1")
315	
316	        # Extract bit slice from the RHS (e.g., usb_cmd_value[3:0])
317	        bit_slice = ""
318	        slice_m = re.search(r'usb_cmd_value(\[\d+(?::\d+)?\])', rhs)
319	        if slice_m:
320	            bit_slice = slice_m.group(1)
321	        elif "usb_cmd_value" in rhs:
322	            bit_slice = "[15:0]"  # full width
323	
324	        bit_width = _parse_bit_slice(bit_slice) if bit_slice else 0
325	
326	        opcodes[value] = OpcodeEntry(
327	            name=register,
328	            value=value,
329	            register=register,
330	            bit_slice=bit_slice,
331	            bit_width=bit_width,
332	            is_pulse=is_pulse,
333	        )
334	
335	    # Pattern 2: begin...end blocks — 8'hXX: begin ... register <= ... end
336	    # These are used for opcodes with validation logic (e.g., 0x15 clamp)
337	    for m in re.finditer(
338	        r"8'h([0-9a-fA-F]{2})\s*:\s*begin\b(.*?)end\b",
339	        text, re.DOTALL
340	    ):
341	        value = int(m.group(1), 16)
342	        if value in opcodes:
343	            continue  # Already captured by pattern 1
344	        body = m.group(2)
345	
346	        # Find the first register assignment (host_xxx <=)
347	        assign_m = re.search(r'(host_\w+)\s*<=\s*(.+?);', body)
348	        if not assign_m:
349	            continue
350	
351	        register = assign_m.group(1)
352	        rhs = assign_m.group(2).strip()
353	
354	        bit_slice = ""
355	        slice_m = re.search(r'usb_cmd_value(\[\d+(?::\d+)?\])', body)
356	        if slice_m:
357	            bit_slice = slice_m.group(1)
358	        elif "usb_cmd_value" in body:
359	            bit_slice = "[15:0]"
360	
361	        bit_width = _parse_bit_slice(bit_slice) if bit_slice else 0
362	
363	        opcodes[value] = OpcodeEntry(
364	            name=register,
365	            value=value,
366	            register=register,
367	            bit_slice=bit_slice,
368	            bit_width=bit_width,
369	            is_pulse=False,
370	        )
371	
372	    return opcodes
373	
374	
375	def parse_verilog_reset_defaults(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, int]:
376	    """
377	    Parse the reset block from radar_system_top.v.
378	    Returns {register_name: reset_value}.
379	    """
380	    if filepath is None:
381	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v"
382	    text = filepath.read_text()
383	
384	    defaults: dict[str, int] = {}
385	
386	    # Match patterns like: host_radar_mode <= 2'b01;
387	    # Also: host_detect_threshold <= 16'd10000;
388	    for m in re.finditer(
389	        r'(host_\w+)\s*<=\s*(\d+\'[bdho][0-9a-fA-F_]+|\d+)\s*;',
390	        text
391	    ):
392	        reg = m.group(1)
393	        val_str = m.group(2)
394	
395	        # Parse Verilog literal
396	        if "'" in val_str:
397	            base_char = val_str.split("'")[1][0].lower()
398	            digits = val_str.split("'")[1][1:].replace("_", "")
399	            base = {"b": 2, "d": 10, "h": 16, "o": 8}[base_char]
400	            value = int(digits, base)
401	        else:
402	            value = int(val_str)
403	
404	        # Only keep first occurrence (the reset block comes before the
405	        # opcode decode which also has <= assignments)
406	        if reg not in defaults:
407	            defaults[reg] = value
408	
409	    return defaults
410	
411	
412	def parse_verilog_register_widths(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, int]:
413	    """
414	    Parse register declarations from radar_system_top.v.
415	    Returns {register_name: bit_width}.
416	    """
417	    if filepath is None:
418	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v"
419	    text = filepath.read_text()
420	
421	    widths: dict[str, int] = {}
422	
423	    # Match: reg [15:0] host_detect_threshold;
424	    # Also:  reg        host_trigger_pulse;
425	    for m in re.finditer(
426	        r'reg\s+(?:\[\s*(\d+)\s*:\s*(\d+)\s*\]\s+)?(host_\w+)\s*;',
427	        text
428	    ):
429	        width = int(m.group(1)) - int(m.group(2)) + 1 if m.group(1) is not None else 1
430	        widths[m.group(3)] = width
431	
432	    return widths
433	
434	
435	def parse_verilog_packet_constants(
436	    filepath: Path | None = None,
437	) -> dict[str, PacketConstants]:
438	    """Extract HEADER, FOOTER, STATUS_HEADER, packet size localparams."""
439	    if filepath is None:
440	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
441	    text = filepath.read_text()
442	
443	    def _find(pattern: str) -> int:
444	        m = re.search(pattern, text)
445	        if not m:
446	            raise ValueError(f"Pattern not found in {filepath}: {pattern}")
447	        val = m.group(1)
448	        # Parse Verilog literals: 8'hAA → 0xAA, 5'd11 → 11
449	        vlog_m = re.match(r"\d+'h([0-9a-fA-F]+)", val)
450	        if vlog_m:
451	            return int(vlog_m.group(1), 16)
452	        vlog_m = re.match(r"\d+'d(\d+)", val)
453	        if vlog_m:
454	            return int(vlog_m.group(1))
455	        return int(val, 16) if val.startswith("0x") else int(val)
456	
457	    header_val = _find(r"localparam\s+HEADER\s*=\s*(\d+'h[0-9a-fA-F]+)")
458	    footer_val = _find(r"localparam\s+FOOTER\s*=\s*(\d+'h[0-9a-fA-F]+)")
459	    status_hdr = _find(r"localparam\s+STATUS_HEADER\s*=\s*(\d+'h[0-9a-fA-F]+)")
460	
461	    data_size = _find(r"DATA_PKT_LEN\s*=\s*(\d+'d\d+)")
462	    status_size = _find(r"STATUS_PKT_LEN\s*=\s*(\d+'d\d+)")
463	
464	    return {
465	        "data": PacketConstants(header=header_val, footer=footer_val, size=data_size),
466	        "status": PacketConstants(header=status_hdr, footer=footer_val, size=status_size),
467	    }
468	
469	
470	def count_concat_bits(concat_expr: str, port_widths: dict[str, int]) -> ConcatWidth:
471	    """
472	    Count total bits in a Verilog concatenation expression like:
473	      {8'hFF, 3'b000, status_radar_mode, 5'b00000, status_stream_ctrl, status_cfar_threshold}
474	
475	    Uses port_widths to resolve signal widths. Returns ConcatWidth.
476	    """
477	    # Remove outer braces
478	    inner = concat_expr.strip().strip("{}")
479	    fragments: list[tuple[str, int]] = []
480	    total = 0
481	
482	    for part in re.split(r',\s*', inner):
483	        part = part.strip()
484	        if not part:
485	            continue
486	
487	        # Literal: N'bXXX, N'dXXX, N'hXX, or just a decimal
488	        lit_match = re.match(r"(\d+)'[bdhoBDHO]", part)
489	        if lit_match:
490	            w = int(lit_match.group(1))
491	            fragments.append((part, w))
492	            total += w
493	            continue
494	
495	        # Signal with bit select: sig[M:N] or sig[N]
496	        sel_match = re.match(r'(\w+)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]', part)
497	        if sel_match:
498	            w = int(sel_match.group(2)) - int(sel_match.group(3)) + 1
499	            fragments.append((part, w))
500	            total += w
501	            continue
502	
503	        sel_match = re.match(r'(\w+)\[(\d+)\]', part)
504	        if sel_match:
505	            fragments.append((part, 1))
506	            total += 1
507	            continue
508	
509	        # Bare signal: look up in port_widths
510	        if part in port_widths:
511	            w = port_widths[part]
512	            fragments.append((part, w))
513	            total += w
514	        else:
515	            # Unknown width — flag it
516	            fragments.append((part, -1))
517	            total = -1  # Can't compute
518	            break
519	
520	    return ConcatWidth(
521	        total_bits=total,
522	        target_bits=32,
523	        fragments=fragments,
524	        truncated=total > 32 if total > 0 else False,
525	    )
526	
527	
528	def parse_verilog_status_word_concats(
529	    filepath: Path | None = None,
530	) -> dict[int, str]:
531	    """
532	    Extract the raw concatenation expression for each status_words[N] assignment.
533	    Returns {word_index: concat_expression_string}.
534	    """
535	    if filepath is None:
536	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
537	    text = filepath.read_text()
538	
539	    results: dict[int, str] = {}
540	
541	    # Multi-line concat: status_words[N] <= {... };
542	    # We need to handle multi-line expressions
543	    for m in re.finditer(
544	        r'status_words\[(\d+)\]\s*<=\s*(\{[^;]+\})\s*;',
545	        text, re.DOTALL
546	    ):
547	        idx = int(m.group(1))
548	        expr = m.group(2)
549	        # Strip single-line comments before normalizing whitespace
550	        expr = re.sub(r'//[^\n]*', '', expr)
551	        # Normalize whitespace
552	        expr = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', expr).strip()
553	        results[idx] = expr
554	
555	    return results
556	
557	
558	def get_usb_interface_port_widths(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, int]:
559	    """
560	    Parse port declarations from usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v module header.
561	    Returns {port_name: bit_width}.
562	    """
563	    if filepath is None:
564	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
565	    text = filepath.read_text()
566	
567	    widths: dict[str, int] = {}
568	
569	    # Match: input wire [15:0] status_cfar_threshold,
570	    # Also:  input wire        status_self_test_busy
571	    for m in re.finditer(
572	        r'(?:input|output)\s+(?:wire|reg)\s+(?:\[\s*(\d+)\s*:\s*(\d+)\s*\]\s+)?(\w+)',
573	        text
574	    ):
575	        width = int(m.group(1)) - int(m.group(2)) + 1 if m.group(1) is not None else 1
576	        widths[m.group(3)] = width
577	
578	    return widths
579	
580	
581	def parse_verilog_data_mux(
582	    filepath: Path | None = None,
583	) -> list[DataPacketField]:
584	    """
585	    Parse the data_pkt_byte mux from usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v.
586	    Returns fields with byte positions and signal names.
587	    """
588	    if filepath is None:
589	        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
590	    text = filepath.read_text()
591	
592	    # Find the data mux case block
593	    match = re.search(
594	        r'always\s+@\(\*\)\s+begin\s+case\s*\(wr_byte_idx\)(.*?)endcase',
595	        text, re.DOTALL
596	    )
597	    if not match:
598	        raise ValueError("Could not find data_pkt_byte mux")
599	
600	    mux_body = match.group(1)
601	    entries: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
602	
603	    for m in re.finditer(
604	        r"5'd(\d+)\s*:\s*data_pkt_byte\s*=\s*(.+?);",
605	        mux_body, re.DOTALL
606	    ):
607	        idx = int(m.group(1))
608	        expr = m.group(2).strip()
609	        entries.append((idx, expr))
610	
611	    # Helper: extract the dominant signal name from a mux expression.
612	    # Handles direct refs like ``range_profile_cap[31:24]``, ternaries
613	    # like ``stream_doppler_en ? doppler_real_cap[15:8] : 8'd0``, and
614	    # concat-ternaries like ``stream_cfar_en ? {…, cfar_detection_cap} : …``.
615	    def _extract_signal(expr: str) -> str | None:
616	        # If it's a ternary, use the true-branch to find the data signal
617	        tern = re.match(r'\w+\s*\?\s*(.+?)\s*:\s*.+', expr, re.DOTALL)
618	        target = tern.group(1) if tern else expr
619	        # Look for a known data signal (xxx_cap pattern or cfar_detection_cap)
620	        cap_match = re.search(r'(\w+_cap)\b', target)
621	        if cap_match:
622	            return cap_match.group(1)
623	        # Fall back to first identifier before a bit-select
624	        sig_match = re.match(r'(\w+?)(?:\[|$)', target)
625	        return sig_match.group(1) if sig_match else None
626	
627	    # Group consecutive bytes by signal root name
628	    fields: list[DataPacketField] = []
629	    i = 0
630	    while i < len(entries):
631	        idx, expr = entries[i]
632	        if expr == "HEADER" or expr == "FOOTER":
633	            i += 1
634	            continue
635	
636	        signal = _extract_signal(expr)
637	        if not signal:
638	            i += 1
639	            continue
640	
641	        start_byte = idx
642	        end_byte = idx
643	
644	        # Find consecutive bytes of the same signal
645	        j = i + 1
646	        while j < len(entries):
647	            _next_idx, next_expr = entries[j]
648	            next_sig = _extract_signal(next_expr)
649	            if next_sig == signal:
650	                end_byte = _next_idx
651	                j += 1
652	            else:
653	                break
654	
655	        n_bytes = end_byte - start_byte + 1
656	        fields.append(DataPacketField(
657	            name=signal.replace("_cap", ""),
658	            byte_start=start_byte,
659	            byte_end=end_byte,
660	            width_bits=n_bytes * 8,
661	        ))
662	        i = j
663	
664	    return fields
665	
666	
667	# ===================================================================
668	# STM32 / C layer parser
669	# ===================================================================
670	
671	def parse_stm32_settings_fields(
672	    filepath: Path | None = None,
673	) -> list[SettingsField]:
674	    """
675	    Parse RadarSettings::parseFromUSB to extract field order, offsets, types.
676	    """
677	    if filepath is None:
678	        filepath = MCU_LIB_DIR / "RadarSettings.cpp"
679	
680	    if not filepath.exists():
681	        return []  # MCU code not available (CI might not have it)
682	
683	    text = filepath.read_text(encoding="latin-1")
684	
685	    fields: list[SettingsField] = []
686	
687	    # Look for memcpy + shift patterns that extract doubles and uint32s
688	    # Pattern for doubles: loop reading 8 bytes big-endian
689	    # Pattern for uint32: 4 bytes big-endian
690	    # We'll parse the assignment targets in order
691	
692	    # Find the parseFromUSB function
693	    match = re.search(
694	        r'parseFromUSB\s*\(.*?\)\s*\{(.*?)^\}',
695	        text, re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE
696	    )
697	    if not match:
698	        return fields
699	
700	    body = match.group(1)
701	
702	    # The fields are extracted sequentially from the payload.
703	    # Look for variable assignments that follow the memcpy/extraction pattern.
704	    # Based on known code: extractDouble / extractUint32 patterns
705	    field_names = [
706	        ("system_frequency", 8, "double"),
707	        ("chirp_duration_1", 8, "double"),
708	        ("chirp_duration_2", 8, "double"),
709	        ("chirps_per_position", 4, "uint32_t"),
710	        ("freq_min", 8, "double"),
711	        ("freq_max", 8, "double"),
712	        ("prf1", 8, "double"),
713	        ("prf2", 8, "double"),
714	        ("max_distance", 8, "double"),
715	        ("map_size", 8, "double"),
716	    ]
717	
718	    offset = 0
719	    for name, size, ctype in field_names:
720	        # Verify the field name appears in the function body
721	        if name in body or name.replace("_", "") in body.lower():
722	            fields.append(SettingsField(
723	                name=name, offset=offset, size=size, c_type=ctype
724	            ))
725	        offset += size
726	
727	    return fields
728	
729	
730	def parse_stm32_start_flag(
731	    filepath: Path | None = None,
732	) -> list[int]:
733	    """Parse the USB start flag bytes from USBHandler.cpp."""
734	    if filepath is None:
735	        filepath = MCU_LIB_DIR / "USBHandler.cpp"
736	
737	    if not filepath.exists():
738	        return []
739	
740	    text = filepath.read_text()
741	
742	    # Look for the start flag array, e.g. {23, 46, 158, 237}
743	    match = re.search(r'start_flag.*?=\s*\{([^}]+)\}', text, re.DOTALL)
744	    if not match:
745	        # Try alternate patterns
746	        match = re.search(r'\{(\s*\d+\s*,\s*\d+\s*,\s*\d+\s*,\s*\d+\s*)\}', text)
747	        if not match:
748	            return []
749	
750	    return [int(x.strip()) for x in match.group(1).split(",") if x.strip().isdigit()]
751	
752	
753	# ===================================================================
754	# GPIO parser
755	# ===================================================================
756	
757	def parse_xdc_gpio_pins(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[GpioPin]:
758	    """Parse XDC constraints for DIG_* pin assignments."""
759	    if filepath is None:
760	        filepath = XDC_DIR / "xc7a50t_ftg256.xdc"
761	
762	    if not filepath.exists():
763	        return []
764	
765	    text = filepath.read_text()
766	    pins: list[GpioPin] = []
767	
768	    # Match: set_property PACKAGE_PIN XX [get_ports {signal_name}]
769	    for m in re.finditer(
770	        r'set_property\s+PACKAGE_PIN\s+(\w+)\s+\[get_ports\s+\{?(\w+)\}?\]',
771	        text
772	    ):
773	        pin = m.group(1)
774	        signal = m.group(2)
775	        if any(kw in signal for kw in ("stm32_", "reset_n", "dig_")):
776	            # Determine direction from signal name
777	            if signal in ("stm32_new_chirp", "stm32_new_elevation",
778	                         "stm32_new_azimuth", "stm32_mixers_enable"):
779	                direction = "input"  # FPGA receives these
780	            elif signal == "reset_n":
781	                direction = "input"
782	            else:
783	                direction = "unknown"
784	            pins.append(GpioPin(
785	                name=signal, pin_id=pin, direction=direction, layer="fpga"
786	            ))
787	
788	    return pins
789	
790	
791	def parse_stm32_gpio_init(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[GpioPin]:
792	    """Parse STM32 GPIO initialization for PD8-PD15 directions."""
793	    if filepath is None:
794	        filepath = MCU_CODE_DIR / "main.cpp"
795	
796	    if not filepath.exists():
797	        return []
798	
799	    text = filepath.read_text()
800	    pins: list[GpioPin] = []
801	
802	    # Look for GPIO_InitStruct.Pin and GPIO_InitStruct.Mode patterns
803	    # This is approximate — STM32 HAL GPIO init is complex
804	    # Look for PD8-PD15 configuration (output vs input)
805	
806	    # Pattern: GPIO_PIN_8 | GPIO_PIN_9 ... with Mode = OUTPUT
807	    # We'll find blocks that configure GPIOD pins
808	    for m in re.finditer(
809	        r'GPIO_InitStruct\.Pin\s*=\s*([^;]+);.*?'
810	        r'GPIO_InitStruct\.Mode\s*=\s*(\w+)',
811	        text, re.DOTALL
812	    ):
813	        pin_expr = m.group(1)
814	        mode = m.group(2)
815	
816	        direction = "output" if "OUTPUT" in mode else "input"
817	
818	        # Extract individual pin numbers
819	        for pin_m in re.finditer(r'GPIO_PIN_(\d+)', pin_expr):
820	            pin_num = int(pin_m.group(1))
821	            if 8 <= pin_num <= 15:
822	                pins.append(GpioPin(
823	                    name=f"PD{pin_num}",
824	                    pin_id=f"PD{pin_num}",
825	                    direction=direction,
826	                    layer="stm32"
827	                ))
828	
829	    return pins
830

file.content:
"""
Cross-layer contract parsers.

Extracts interface contracts (opcodes, bit widths, reset defaults, packet
layouts) directly from the source files of each layer:
  - Python GUI:  radar_protocol.py
  - FPGA RTL:    radar_system_top.v, usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v,
                 usb_data_interface.v
  - STM32 MCU:   RadarSettings.cpp, main.cpp

These parsers do NOT define the expected values — they discover what each
layer actually implements, so the test can compare layers against ground
truth and find bugs where both sides are wrong (like the 0x06 phantom
opcode or the status_words[0] 37-bit truncation).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Repository layout (relative to repo root)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
GUI_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "9_Firmware" / "9_3_GUI"
FPGA_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "9_Firmware" / "9_2_FPGA"
MCU_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "9_Firmware" / "9_1_Microcontroller"
MCU_LIB_DIR = MCU_DIR / "9_1_1_C_Cpp_Libraries"
MCU_CODE_DIR = MCU_DIR / "9_1_3_C_Cpp_Code"
XDC_DIR = FPGA_DIR / "constraints"


# ===================================================================
# Data structures
# ===================================================================

@dataclass
class OpcodeEntry:
    """One opcode as declared in a single layer."""
    name: str
    value: int
    register: str = ""      # Verilog register name it writes to
    bit_slice: str = ""     # e.g. "[3:0]", "[15:0]", "[0]"
    bit_width: int = 0      # derived from bit_slice
    reset_default: int | None = None
    is_pulse: bool = False  # True for trigger/request opcodes


@dataclass
class StatusWordField:
    """One field inside a status_words[] entry."""
    name: str
    word_index: int
    msb: int   # bit position in the 32-bit word (0-indexed from LSB)
    lsb: int
    width: int


@dataclass
class DataPacketField:
    """One field in the 11-byte data packet."""
    name: str
    byte_start: int   # first byte index (0 = header)
    byte_end: int     # last byte index (inclusive)
    width_bits: int


@dataclass
class PacketConstants:
    """Header/footer/size constants for a packet type."""
    header: int
    footer: int
    size: int


@dataclass
class SettingsField:
    """One field in the STM32 SET...END settings packet."""
    name: str
    offset: int        # byte offset from start of payload (after "SET")
    size: int          # bytes
    c_type: str        # "double" or "uint32_t"


@dataclass
class GpioPin:
    """A GPIO pin with direction."""
    name: str
    pin_id: str        # e.g. "PD8", "H11"
    direction: str     # "output" or "input"
    layer: str         # "stm32" or "fpga"


@dataclass
class ConcatWidth:
    """Result of counting bits in a Verilog concatenation."""
    total_bits: int
    target_bits: int   # width of the register being assigned to
    fragments: list[tuple[str, int]] = field(default_factory=list)
    truncated: bool = False


# ===================================================================
# Python layer parser
# ===================================================================

def parse_python_opcodes(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[int, OpcodeEntry]:
    """Parse the Opcode enum from radar_protocol.py.
    Returns {opcode_value: OpcodeEntry}.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    # Find the Opcode class body
    match = re.search(r'class Opcode\b.*?(?=\nclass |\Z)', text, re.DOTALL)
    if not match:
        raise ValueError(f"Could not find 'class Opcode' in {filepath}")

    opcodes: dict[int, OpcodeEntry] = {}
    for m in re.finditer(r'(\w+)\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)', match.group()):
        name = m.group(1)
        value = int(m.group(2), 16)
        opcodes[value] = OpcodeEntry(name=name, value=value)
    return opcodes


def parse_python_packet_constants(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, PacketConstants]:
    """Extract HEADER_BYTE, FOOTER_BYTE, STATUS_HEADER_BYTE, packet sizes."""
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    def _find(pattern: str) -> int:
        m = re.search(pattern, text)
        if not m:
            raise ValueError(f"Pattern not found: {pattern}")
        val = m.group(1)
        return int(val, 16) if val.startswith("0x") else int(val)

    header = _find(r'HEADER_BYTE\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)')
    footer = _find(r'FOOTER_BYTE\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)')
    status_header = _find(r'STATUS_HEADER_BYTE\s*=\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)')
    data_size = _find(r'DATA_PACKET_SIZE\s*=\s*(\d+)')
    status_size = _find(r'STATUS_PACKET_SIZE\s*=\s*(\d+)')

    return {
        "data": PacketConstants(header=header, footer=footer, size=data_size),
        "status": PacketConstants(header=status_header, footer=footer, size=status_size),
    }


def parse_python_data_packet_fields(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[DataPacketField]:
    """
    Extract byte offsets from parse_data_packet() by finding struct.unpack_from calls.
    Returns fields in byte order.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    # Find parse_data_packet method body
    match = re.search(
        r'def parse_data_packet\(.*?\).*?(?=\n    @|\n    def |\nclass |\Z)',
        text, re.DOTALL
    )
    if not match:
        raise ValueError("Could not find parse_data_packet()")

    body = match.group()
    fields: list[DataPacketField] = []

    # Match patterns like: range_q = _to_signed16(struct.unpack_from(">H", raw, 1)[0])
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'(\w+)\s*=\s*_to_signed16\(struct\.unpack_from\("(>[HIBhib])", raw, (\d+)\)',
        body
    ):
        name = m.group(1)
        fmt = m.group(2)
        offset = int(m.group(3))
        fmt_char = fmt[-1].upper()
        size = {"H": 2, "I": 4, "B": 1}[fmt_char]
        fields.append(DataPacketField(
            name=name, byte_start=offset,
            byte_end=offset + size - 1,
            width_bits=size * 8
        ))

    # Match detection = raw[9] & 0x01  (direct access)
    for m in re.finditer(r'(\w+)\s*=\s*raw\[(\d+)\]\s*&\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)', body):
        name = m.group(1)
        offset = int(m.group(2))
        fields.append(DataPacketField(
            name=name, byte_start=offset, byte_end=offset, width_bits=1
        ))

    # Match intermediate variable pattern: var = raw[N], then field = var & MASK
    for m in re.finditer(r'(\w+)\s*=\s*raw\[(\d+)\]', body):
        var_name = m.group(1)
        offset = int(m.group(2))
        # Find fields derived from this intermediate variable
        for m2 in re.finditer(
            rf'(\w+)\s*=\s*(?:\({var_name}\s*>>\s*\d+\)\s*&|{var_name}\s*&)\s*'
            r'(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)',
            body,
        ):
            name = m2.group(1)
            # Skip if already captured by direct raw[] access pattern
            if not any(f.name == name for f in fields):
                fields.append(DataPacketField(
                    name=name, byte_start=offset, byte_end=offset,
                    width_bits=1
                ))

    fields.sort(key=lambda f: f.byte_start)
    return fields


def parse_python_status_fields(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[StatusWordField]:
    """
    Extract bit shift/mask operations from parse_status_packet().
    Returns the fields with word index and bit positions as Python sees them.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    match = re.search(
        r'def parse_status_packet\(.*?\).*?(?=\n    @|\n    def |\nclass |\Z)',
        text, re.DOTALL
    )
    if not match:
        raise ValueError("Could not find parse_status_packet()")

    body = match.group()
    fields: list[StatusWordField] = []

    # Pattern: sr.field = (words[N] >> S) & MASK  # noqa: ERA001
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'sr\.(\w+)\s*=\s*\(words\[(\d+)\]\s*>>\s*(\d+)\)\s*&\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)',
        body
    ):
        name = m.group(1)
        word_idx = int(m.group(2))
        shift = int(m.group(3))
        mask_str = m.group(4)
        mask = int(mask_str, 16) if mask_str.startswith("0x") else int(mask_str)
        width = mask.bit_length()
        fields.append(StatusWordField(
            name=name, word_index=word_idx,
            msb=shift + width - 1, lsb=shift, width=width
        ))

    # Pattern: sr.field = words[N] & MASK  (no shift)
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'sr\.(\w+)\s*=\s*words\[(\d+)\]\s*&\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+|\d+)',
        body
    ):
        name = m.group(1)
        word_idx = int(m.group(2))
        mask_str = m.group(3)
        mask = int(mask_str, 16) if mask_str.startswith("0x") else int(mask_str)
        width = mask.bit_length()
        # Skip if already captured by the shift pattern
        if not any(f.name == name and f.word_index == word_idx for f in fields):
            fields.append(StatusWordField(
                name=name, word_index=word_idx,
                msb=width - 1, lsb=0, width=width
            ))

    return fields


# ===================================================================
# Verilog layer parser
# ===================================================================

def _parse_bit_slice(s: str) -> int:
    """Parse '[15:0]' -> 16, '[0]' -> 1, '' -> 16 (full cmd_value)."""
    m = re.match(r'\[(\d+):(\d+)\]', s)
    if m:
        return int(m.group(1)) - int(m.group(2)) + 1
    m = re.match(r'\[(\d+)\]', s)
    if m:
        return 1
    return 16  # default: full 16-bit cmd_value


def parse_verilog_opcodes(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[int, OpcodeEntry]:
    """
    Parse the opcode case statement from radar_system_top.v.
    Returns {opcode_value: OpcodeEntry}.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    # Find the command decode case block
    # Pattern: case statement with 8'hXX opcodes
    opcodes: dict[int, OpcodeEntry] = {}

    # Pattern 1: Simple assignment — 8'hXX: register <= rhs;
    for m in re.finditer(
        r"8'h([0-9a-fA-F]{2})\s*:\s*(\w+)\s*<=\s*(.*?)(?:;|$)",
        text, re.MULTILINE
    ):
        value = int(m.group(1), 16)
        register = m.group(2)
        rhs = m.group(3).strip()

        # Determine if it's a pulse (assigned literal 1)
        is_pulse = rhs in ("1", "1'b1")

        # Extract bit slice from the RHS (e.g., usb_cmd_value[3:0])
        bit_slice = ""
        slice_m = re.search(r'usb_cmd_value(\[\d+(?::\d+)?\])', rhs)
        if slice_m:
            bit_slice = slice_m.group(1)
        elif "usb_cmd_value" in rhs:
            bit_slice = "[15:0]"  # full width

        bit_width = _parse_bit_slice(bit_slice) if bit_slice else 0

        opcodes[value] = OpcodeEntry(
            name=register,
            value=value,
            register=register,
            bit_slice=bit_slice,
            bit_width=bit_width,
            is_pulse=is_pulse,
        )

    # Pattern 2: begin...end blocks — 8'hXX: begin ... register <= ... end
    # These are used for opcodes with validation logic (e.g., 0x15 clamp)
    for m in re.finditer(
        r"8'h([0-9a-fA-F]{2})\s*:\s*begin\b(.*?)end\b",
        text, re.DOTALL
    ):
        value = int(m.group(1), 16)
        if value in opcodes:
            continue  # Already captured by pattern 1
        body = m.group(2)

        # Find the first register assignment (host_xxx <=)
        assign_m = re.search(r'(host_\w+)\s*<=\s*(.+?);', body)
        if not assign_m:
            continue

        register = assign_m.group(1)
        rhs = assign_m.group(2).strip()

        bit_slice = ""
        slice_m = re.search(r'usb_cmd_value(\[\d+(?::\d+)?\])', body)
        if slice_m:
            bit_slice = slice_m.group(1)
        elif "usb_cmd_value" in body:
            bit_slice = "[15:0]"

        bit_width = _parse_bit_slice(bit_slice) if bit_slice else 0

        opcodes[value] = OpcodeEntry(
            name=register,
            value=value,
            register=register,
            bit_slice=bit_slice,
            bit_width=bit_width,
            is_pulse=False,
        )

    return opcodes


def parse_verilog_reset_defaults(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, int]:
    """
    Parse the reset block from radar_system_top.v.
    Returns {register_name: reset_value}.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    defaults: dict[str, int] = {}

    # Match patterns like: host_radar_mode <= 2'b01;
    # Also: host_detect_threshold <= 16'd10000;
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'(host_\w+)\s*<=\s*(\d+\'[bdho][0-9a-fA-F_]+|\d+)\s*;',
        text
    ):
        reg = m.group(1)
        val_str = m.group(2)

        # Parse Verilog literal
        if "'" in val_str:
            base_char = val_str.split("'")[1][0].lower()
            digits = val_str.split("'")[1][1:].replace("_", "")
            base = {"b": 2, "d": 10, "h": 16, "o": 8}[base_char]
            value = int(digits, base)
        else:
            value = int(val_str)

        # Only keep first occurrence (the reset block comes before the
        # opcode decode which also has <= assignments)
        if reg not in defaults:
            defaults[reg] = value

    return defaults


def parse_verilog_register_widths(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, int]:
    """
    Parse register declarations from radar_system_top.v.
    Returns {register_name: bit_width}.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    widths: dict[str, int] = {}

    # Match: reg [15:0] host_detect_threshold;
    # Also:  reg        host_trigger_pulse;
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'reg\s+(?:\[\s*(\d+)\s*:\s*(\d+)\s*\]\s+)?(host_\w+)\s*;',
        text
    ):
        width = int(m.group(1)) - int(m.group(2)) + 1 if m.group(1) is not None else 1
        widths[m.group(3)] = width

    return widths


def parse_verilog_packet_constants(
    filepath: Path | None = None,
) -> dict[str, PacketConstants]:
    """Extract HEADER, FOOTER, STATUS_HEADER, packet size localparams."""
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    def _find(pattern: str) -> int:
        m = re.search(pattern, text)
        if not m:
            raise ValueError(f"Pattern not found in {filepath}: {pattern}")
        val = m.group(1)
        # Parse Verilog literals: 8'hAA → 0xAA, 5'd11 → 11
        vlog_m = re.match(r"\d+'h([0-9a-fA-F]+)", val)
        if vlog_m:
            return int(vlog_m.group(1), 16)
        vlog_m = re.match(r"\d+'d(\d+)", val)
        if vlog_m:
            return int(vlog_m.group(1))
        return int(val, 16) if val.startswith("0x") else int(val)

    header_val = _find(r"localparam\s+HEADER\s*=\s*(\d+'h[0-9a-fA-F]+)")
    footer_val = _find(r"localparam\s+FOOTER\s*=\s*(\d+'h[0-9a-fA-F]+)")
    status_hdr = _find(r"localparam\s+STATUS_HEADER\s*=\s*(\d+'h[0-9a-fA-F]+)")

    data_size = _find(r"DATA_PKT_LEN\s*=\s*(\d+'d\d+)")
    status_size = _find(r"STATUS_PKT_LEN\s*=\s*(\d+'d\d+)")

    return {
        "data": PacketConstants(header=header_val, footer=footer_val, size=data_size),
        "status": PacketConstants(header=status_hdr, footer=footer_val, size=status_size),
    }


def count_concat_bits(concat_expr: str, port_widths: dict[str, int]) -> ConcatWidth:
    """
    Count total bits in a Verilog concatenation expression like:
      {8'hFF, 3'b000, status_radar_mode, 5'b00000, status_stream_ctrl, status_cfar_threshold}

    Uses port_widths to resolve signal widths. Returns ConcatWidth.
    """
    # Remove outer braces
    inner = concat_expr.strip().strip("{}")
    fragments: list[tuple[str, int]] = []
    total = 0

    for part in re.split(r',\s*', inner):
        part = part.strip()
        if not part:
            continue

        # Literal: N'bXXX, N'dXXX, N'hXX, or just a decimal
        lit_match = re.match(r"(\d+)'[bdhoBDHO]", part)
        if lit_match:
            w = int(lit_match.group(1))
            fragments.append((part, w))
            total += w
            continue

        # Signal with bit select: sig[M:N] or sig[N]
        sel_match = re.match(r'(\w+)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]', part)
        if sel_match:
            w = int(sel_match.group(2)) - int(sel_match.group(3)) + 1
            fragments.append((part, w))
            total += w
            continue

        sel_match = re.match(r'(\w+)\[(\d+)\]', part)
        if sel_match:
            fragments.append((part, 1))
            total += 1
            continue

        # Bare signal: look up in port_widths
        if part in port_widths:
            w = port_widths[part]
            fragments.append((part, w))
            total += w
        else:
            # Unknown width — flag it
            fragments.append((part, -1))
            total = -1  # Can't compute
            break

    return ConcatWidth(
        total_bits=total,
        target_bits=32,
        fragments=fragments,
        truncated=total > 32 if total > 0 else False,
    )


def parse_verilog_status_word_concats(
    filepath: Path | None = None,
) -> dict[int, str]:
    """
    Extract the raw concatenation expression for each status_words[N] assignment.
    Returns {word_index: concat_expression_string}.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    results: dict[int, str] = {}

    # Multi-line concat: status_words[N] <= {... };
    # We need to handle multi-line expressions
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'status_words\[(\d+)\]\s*<=\s*(\{[^;]+\})\s*;',
        text, re.DOTALL
    ):
        idx = int(m.group(1))
        expr = m.group(2)
        # Strip single-line comments before normalizing whitespace
        expr = re.sub(r'//[^\n]*', '', expr)
        # Normalize whitespace
        expr = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', expr).strip()
        results[idx] = expr

    return results


def get_usb_interface_port_widths(filepath: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, int]:
    """
    Parse port declarations from usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v module header.
    Returns {port_name: bit_width}.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    widths: dict[str, int] = {}

    # Match: input wire [15:0] status_cfar_threshold,
    # Also:  input wire        status_self_test_busy
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'(?:input|output)\s+(?:wire|reg)\s+(?:\[\s*(\d+)\s*:\s*(\d+)\s*\]\s+)?(\w+)',
        text
    ):
        width = int(m.group(1)) - int(m.group(2)) + 1 if m.group(1) is not None else 1
        widths[m.group(3)] = width

    return widths


def parse_verilog_data_mux(
    filepath: Path | None = None,
) -> list[DataPacketField]:
    """
    Parse the data_pkt_byte mux from usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v.
    Returns fields with byte positions and signal names.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
    text = filepath.read_text()

    # Find the data mux case block
    match = re.search(
        r'always\s+@\(\*\)\s+begin\s+case\s*\(wr_byte_idx\)(.*?)endcase',
        text, re.DOTALL
    )
    if not match:
        raise ValueError("Could not find data_pkt_byte mux")

    mux_body = match.group(1)
    entries: list[tuple[int, str]] = []

    for m in re.finditer(
        r"5'd(\d+)\s*:\s*data_pkt_byte\s*=\s*(.+?);",
        mux_body, re.DOTALL
    ):
        idx = int(m.group(1))
        expr = m.group(2).strip()
        entries.append((idx, expr))

    # Helper: extract the dominant signal name from a mux expression.
    # Handles direct refs like ``range_profile_cap[31:24]``, ternaries
    # like ``stream_doppler_en ? doppler_real_cap[15:8] : 8'd0``, and
    # concat-ternaries like ``stream_cfar_en ? {…, cfar_detection_cap} : …``.
    def _extract_signal(expr: str) -> str | None:
        # If it's a ternary, use the true-branch to find the data signal
        tern = re.match(r'\w+\s*\?\s*(.+?)\s*:\s*.+', expr, re.DOTALL)
        target = tern.group(1) if tern else expr
        # Look for a known data signal (xxx_cap pattern or cfar_detection_cap)
        cap_match = re.search(r'(\w+_cap)\b', target)
        if cap_match:
            return cap_match.group(1)
        # Fall back to first identifier before a bit-select
        sig_match = re.match(r'(\w+?)(?:\[|$)', target)
        return sig_match.group(1) if sig_match else None

    # Group consecutive bytes by signal root name
    fields: list[DataPacketField] = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(entries):
        idx, expr = entries[i]
        if expr == "HEADER" or expr == "FOOTER":
            i += 1
            continue

        signal = _extract_signal(expr)
        if not signal:
            i += 1
            continue

        start_byte = idx
        end_byte = idx

        # Find consecutive bytes of the same signal
        j = i + 1
        while j < len(entries):
            _next_idx, next_expr = entries[j]
            next_sig = _extract_signal(next_expr)
            if next_sig == signal:
                end_byte = _next_idx
                j += 1
            else:
                break

        n_bytes = end_byte - start_byte + 1
        fields.append(DataPacketField(
            name=signal.replace("_cap", ""),
            byte_start=start_byte,
            byte_end=end_byte,
            width_bits=n_bytes * 8,
        ))
        i = j

    return fields


# ===================================================================
# STM32 / C layer parser
# ===================================================================

def parse_stm32_settings_fields(
    filepath: Path | None = None,
) -> list[SettingsField]:
    """
    Parse RadarSettings::parseFromUSB to extract field order, offsets, types.
    """
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = MCU_LIB_DIR / "RadarSettings.cpp"

    if not filepath.exists():
        return []  # MCU code not available (CI might not have it)

    text = filepath.read_text(encoding="latin-1")

    fields: list[SettingsField] = []

    # Look for memcpy + shift patterns that extract doubles and uint32s
    # Pattern for doubles: loop reading 8 bytes big-endian
    # Pattern for uint32: 4 bytes big-endian
    # We'll parse the assignment targets in order

    # Find the parseFromUSB function
    match = re.search(
        r'parseFromUSB\s*\(.*?\)\s*\{(.*?)^\}',
        text, re.DOTALL | re.MULTILINE
    )
    if not match:
        return fields

    body = match.group(1)

    # The fields are extracted sequentially from the payload.
    # Look for variable assignments that follow the memcpy/extraction pattern.
    # Based on known code: extractDouble / extractUint32 patterns
    field_names = [
        ("system_frequency", 8, "double"),
        ("chirp_duration_1", 8, "double"),
        ("chirp_duration_2", 8, "double"),
        ("chirps_per_position", 4, "uint32_t"),
        ("freq_min", 8, "double"),
        ("freq_max", 8, "double"),
        ("prf1", 8, "double"),
        ("prf2", 8, "double"),
        ("max_distance", 8, "double"),
        ("map_size", 8, "double"),
    ]

    offset = 0
    for name, size, ctype in field_names:
        # Verify the field name appears in the function body
        if name in body or name.replace("_", "") in body.lower():
            fields.append(SettingsField(
                name=name, offset=offset, size=size, c_type=ctype
            ))
        offset += size

    return fields


def parse_stm32_start_flag(
    filepath: Path | None = None,
) -> list[int]:
    """Parse the USB start flag bytes from USBHandler.cpp."""
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = MCU_LIB_DIR / "USBHandler.cpp"

    if not filepath.exists():
        return []

    text = filepath.read_text()

    # Look for the start flag array, e.g. {23, 46, 158, 237}
    match = re.search(r'start_flag.*?=\s*\{([^}]+)\}', text, re.DOTALL)
    if not match:
        # Try alternate patterns
        match = re.search(r'\{(\s*\d+\s*,\s*\d+\s*,\s*\d+\s*,\s*\d+\s*)\}', text)
        if not match:
            return []

    return [int(x.strip()) for x in match.group(1).split(",") if x.strip().isdigit()]


# ===================================================================
# GPIO parser
# ===================================================================

def parse_xdc_gpio_pins(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[GpioPin]:
    """Parse XDC constraints for DIG_* pin assignments."""
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = XDC_DIR / "xc7a50t_ftg256.xdc"

    if not filepath.exists():
        return []

    text = filepath.read_text()
    pins: list[GpioPin] = []

    # Match: set_property PACKAGE_PIN XX [get_ports {signal_name}]
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'set_property\s+PACKAGE_PIN\s+(\w+)\s+\[get_ports\s+\{?(\w+)\}?\]',
        text
    ):
        pin = m.group(1)
        signal = m.group(2)
        if any(kw in signal for kw in ("stm32_", "reset_n", "dig_")):
            # Determine direction from signal name
            if signal in ("stm32_new_chirp", "stm32_new_elevation",
                         "stm32_new_azimuth", "stm32_mixers_enable"):
                direction = "input"  # FPGA receives these
            elif signal == "reset_n":
                direction = "input"
            else:
                direction = "unknown"
            pins.append(GpioPin(
                name=signal, pin_id=pin, direction=direction, layer="fpga"
            ))

    return pins


def parse_stm32_gpio_init(filepath: Path | None = None) -> list[GpioPin]:
    """Parse STM32 GPIO initialization for PD8-PD15 directions."""
    if filepath is None:
        filepath = MCU_CODE_DIR / "main.cpp"

    if not filepath.exists():
        return []

    text = filepath.read_text()
    pins: list[GpioPin] = []

    # Look for GPIO_InitStruct.Pin and GPIO_InitStruct.Mode patterns
    # This is approximate — STM32 HAL GPIO init is complex
    # Look for PD8-PD15 configuration (output vs input)

    # Pattern: GPIO_PIN_8 | GPIO_PIN_9 ... with Mode = OUTPUT
    # We'll find blocks that configure GPIOD pins
    for m in re.finditer(
        r'GPIO_InitStruct\.Pin\s*=\s*([^;]+);.*?'
        r'GPIO_InitStruct\.Mode\s*=\s*(\w+)',
        text, re.DOTALL
    ):
        pin_expr = m.group(1)
        mode = m.group(2)

        direction = "output" if "OUTPUT" in mode else "input"

        # Extract individual pin numbers
        for pin_m in re.finditer(r'GPIO_PIN_(\d+)', pin_expr):
            pin_num = int(pin_m.group(1))
            if 8 <= pin_num <= 15:
                pins.append(GpioPin(
                    name=f"PD{pin_num}",
                    pin_id=f"PD{pin_num}",
                    direction=direction,
                    layer="stm32"
                ))

    return pins

content.content:
1	"""
2	Cross-Layer Contract Tests
3	==========================
4	Single pytest file orchestrating three tiers of verification:
5	
6	Tier 1 — Static Contract Parsing:
7	  Compares Python, Verilog, and C source code at parse-time to catch
8	  opcode mismatches, bit-width errors, packet constant drift, and
9	  layout bugs like the status_words[0] 37-bit truncation.
10	
11	Tier 2 — Verilog Cosimulation (iverilog):
12	  Compiles and runs tb_cross_layer_ft2232h.v, then parses its output
13	  files (cmd_results.txt, data_packet.txt, status_packet.txt) and
14	  runs Python parsers on the captured bytes to verify round-trip
15	  correctness.
16	
17	Tier 3 — C Stub Execution:
18	  Compiles stm32_settings_stub.cpp, generates a binary settings
19	  packet from Python, runs the stub, and verifies all parsed field
20	  values match.
21	
22	The goal is to find UNKNOWN bugs by testing each layer against
23	independently-derived ground truth — not just checking that two
24	layers agree (because both could be wrong).
25	"""
26

81	def _strip_cxx_comments_and_strings(src: str) -> str:
82	    """Return src with all C/C++ comments and string/char literals removed.
83	
84	    Tokenising state machine with four states:
85	      * CODE              — default; watches for `"`, `'`, `//`, `/*`
86	      * STRING ("...")    — handles `\\"` and `\\\\` escapes
87	      * CHAR   ('...')    — handles `\\'` and `\\\\` escapes
88	      * LINE_COMMENT      — until next `\\n`
89	      * BLOCK_COMMENT     — until next `*/`
90	
91	    Used by test_vm_gain_table_is_not_reintroduced to ensure the substring
92	    "VM_GAIN" appearing only inside an explanatory comment or a string
93	    literal does NOT count as code reintroduction. We replace stripped
94	    regions with a single space so token boundaries (and line counts, by
95	    approximation — newlines preserved) are not collapsed.
96	    """
97	    out: list[str] = []
98	    i = 0
99	    n = len(src)
100	    CODE, STRING, CHAR, LINE_C, BLOCK_C = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
101	    state = CODE
102	    while i < n:
103	        c = src[i]
104	        nxt = src[i + 1] if i + 1 < n else ""
105	        if state == CODE:
106	            if c == "/" and nxt == "/":
107	                state = LINE_C
108	                i += 2
109	            elif c == "/" and nxt == "*":
110	                state = BLOCK_C
111	                i += 2
112	            elif c == '"':
113	                state = STRING
114	                i += 1
115	            elif c == "'":
116	                state = CHAR
117	                i += 1
118	            else:
119	                out.append(c)
120	                i += 1
121	        elif state == STRING:
122	            if c == "\\" and i + 1 < n:
123	                i += 2  # skip escape pair (handles \" and \\)
124	            elif c == '"':
125	                state = CODE
126	                i += 1
127	            else:
128	                i += 1
129	        elif state == CHAR:
130	            if c == "\\" and i + 1 < n:
131	                i += 2
132	            elif c == "'":
133	                state = CODE
134	                i += 1
135	            else:
136	                i += 1
137	        elif state == LINE_C:
138	            if c == "\n":
139	                out.append("\n")  # preserve line numbering
140	                state = CODE
141	            i += 1
142	        elif state == BLOCK_C:
143	            if c == "*" and nxt == "/":
144	                state = CODE
145	                i += 2
146	            else:
147	                if c == "\n":
148	                    out.append("\n")
149	                i += 1
150	    return "".join(out)
151

153	def _parse_hex_results(text: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
154	    """Parse space-separated hex lines from TB output files."""
155	    rows = []
156	    for line in text.strip().splitlines():
157	        line = line.strip()
158	        if not line or line.startswith("#"):
159	            continue
160	        rows.append(line.split())
161	    return rows

237	class TestTier1OpcodeContract:
238	    """Verify Python and Verilog opcode sets match ground truth."""
239	
240	    def test_python_opcodes_match_ground_truth(self):
241	        """Every Python Opcode must exist in ground truth with correct value."""
242	        py_opcodes = cp.parse_python_opcodes()
243	        for val, entry in py_opcodes.items():
244	            assert val in GROUND_TRUTH_OPCODES, (
245	                f"Python Opcode {entry.name}=0x{val:02X} not in ground truth! "
246	                f"Possible phantom opcode (like the 0x06 incident)."
247	            )
248	
249	    def test_ground_truth_opcodes_in_python(self):
250	        """Every ground truth opcode must have a Python enum entry."""
251	        py_opcodes = cp.parse_python_opcodes()
252	        for val, (reg, _width) in GROUND_TRUTH_OPCODES.items():
253	            assert val in py_opcodes, (
254	                f"Ground truth opcode 0x{val:02X} ({reg}) missing from Python Opcode enum."
255	            )
256	
257	    def test_verilog_opcodes_match_ground_truth(self):
258	        """Every Verilog case entry must exist in ground truth."""
259	        v_opcodes = cp.parse_verilog_opcodes()
260	        for val, entry in v_opcodes.items():
261	            assert val in GROUND_TRUTH_OPCODES, (
262	                f"Verilog opcode 0x{val:02X} ({entry.register}) not in ground truth."
263	            )
264	
265	    def test_ground_truth_opcodes_in_verilog(self):
266	        """Every ground truth opcode must have a Verilog case entry."""
267	        v_opcodes = cp.parse_verilog_opcodes()
268	        for val, (reg, _width) in GROUND_TRUTH_OPCODES.items():
269	            assert val in v_opcodes, (
270	                f"Ground truth opcode 0x{val:02X} ({reg}) missing from Verilog case statement."
271	            )
272	
273	    def test_python_verilog_bidirectional_match(self):
274	        """Python and Verilog must have the same set of opcode values."""
275	        py_set = set(cp.parse_python_opcodes().keys())
276	        v_set = set(cp.parse_verilog_opcodes().keys())
277	        py_only = py_set - v_set
278	        v_only = v_set - py_set
279	        assert not py_only, f"Opcodes in Python but not Verilog: {[hex(x) for x in py_only]}"
280	        assert not v_only, f"Opcodes in Verilog but not Python: {[hex(x) for x in v_only]}"
281	
282	    def test_verilog_register_names_match(self):
283	        """Verilog case target registers must match ground truth names."""
284	        v_opcodes = cp.parse_verilog_opcodes()
285	        for val, (expected_reg, _) in GROUND_TRUTH_OPCODES.items():
286	            if val in v_opcodes:
287	                actual_reg = v_opcodes[val].register
288	                assert actual_reg == expected_reg, (
289	                    f"Opcode 0x{val:02X}: Verilog writes to '{actual_reg}' "
290	                    f"but ground truth says '{expected_reg}'"
291	                )

325	class TestTier1StatusWordTruncation:
326	    """Verify each status_words[] concatenation is exactly 32 bits."""
327	
328	    def test_status_words_concat_widths_ft2232h(self):
329	        """Each status_words[] concat must be EXACTLY 32 bits."""
330	        port_widths = cp.get_usb_interface_port_widths(
331	            cp.FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
332	        )
333	        concats = cp.parse_verilog_status_word_concats(
334	            cp.FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v"
335	        )
336	
337	        for idx, expr in concats.items():
338	            result = cp.count_concat_bits(expr, port_widths)
339	            if result.total_bits < 0:
340	                pytest.skip(f"status_words[{idx}]: unknown signal width")
341	            assert result.total_bits == 32, (
342	                f"status_words[{idx}] is {result.total_bits} bits, not 32! "
343	                f"{'TRUNCATION' if result.total_bits > 32 else 'UNDERFLOW'} BUG. "
344	                f"Fragments: {result.fragments}"
345	            )

348	        """Same check for the FT601 interface (same bug expected)."""
349	        ft601_path = cp.FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface.v"
350	        if not ft601_path.exists():
351	            pytest.skip("FT601 interface file not found")
352	
353	        port_widths = cp.get_usb_interface_port_widths(ft601_path)
354	        concats = cp.parse_verilog_status_word_concats(ft601_path)
355	
356	        for idx, expr in concats.items():
357	            result = cp.count_concat_bits(expr, port_widths)
358	            if result.total_bits < 0:
359	                pytest.skip(f"status_words[{idx}]: unknown signal width")
360	            assert result.total_bits == 32, (
361	                f"FT601 status_words[{idx}] is {result.total_bits} bits, not 32! "
362	                f"{'TRUNCATION' if result.total_bits > 32 else 'UNDERFLOW'} BUG. "
363	                f"Fragments: {result.fragments}"

367	class TestTier1StatusFieldPositions:
368	    """Verify Python status parser bit positions match Verilog layout."""
369	
370	    def test_python_status_mode_position(self):
371	        """
372	        Verify Python reads radar_mode at the correct bit position matching
373	        the Verilog status_words[0] layout:
374	        {0xFF[31:24], mode[23:22], stream[21:19], 3'b000[18:16], threshold[15:0]}
375	        """
376	        # Get what Python thinks
377	        py_fields = cp.parse_python_status_fields()
378	        mode_field = next((f for f in py_fields if f.name == "radar_mode"), None)
379	        assert mode_field is not None, "radar_mode not found in parse_status_packet"
380	
381	        # Ground truth: mode is at bits [23:22], so LSB = 22
382	        expected_shift = 22
383	        actual_shift = mode_field.lsb
384	
385	        assert actual_shift == expected_shift, (
386	            f"Python reads radar_mode at bit {actual_shift} "
387	            f"but Verilog status_words[0] has mode at bit {expected_shift}."
388	        )

446	class TestTier1AgcCrossLayerInvariant:
447	    """
448	    Verify AGC enable/disable is consistent across FPGA, MCU, and GUI layers.
449	
450	    System-level invariant: the FPGA register host_agc_enable is the single
451	    source of truth for AGC state. It propagates to MCU via DIG_6 GPIO and
452	    to GUI via status word 4 bit[11]. At boot, all layers must agree AGC=OFF.
453	    At runtime, the MCU must read DIG_6 every frame to sync its outer-loop AGC.
454	    """
455	
456	    def test_fpga_dig6_drives_agc_enable(self):
457	        """FPGA must drive gpio_dig6 from host_agc_enable, NOT tied low."""
458	        rtl = (cp.FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v").read_text()
459	        # Must find: assign gpio_dig6 = host_agc_enable;
460	        assert re.search(
461	            r'assign\s+gpio_dig6\s*=\s*host_agc_enable\s*;', rtl
462	        ), "gpio_dig6 must be driven by host_agc_enable (not tied low)"
463	        # Must NOT have the old tied-low pattern
464	        assert not re.search(
465	            r"assign\s+gpio_dig6\s*=\s*1'b0\s*;", rtl
466	        ), "gpio_dig6 must NOT be tied low — it carries AGC enable"
467	
468	    def test_fpga_agc_enable_boot_default_off(self):
469	        """FPGA host_agc_enable must reset to 0 (AGC off at boot)."""
470	        v_defaults = cp.parse_verilog_reset_defaults()
471	        assert "host_agc_enable" in v_defaults, (
472	            "host_agc_enable not found in reset block"
473	        )
474	        assert v_defaults["host_agc_enable"] == 0, (
475	            f"host_agc_enable reset default is {v_defaults['host_agc_enable']}, "
476	            "expected 0 (AGC off at boot)"
477	        )
478	
479	    def test_mcu_agc_constructor_default_off(self):
480	        """MCU ADAR1000_AGC constructor must default enabled=false."""
481	        agc_cpp = (cp.MCU_LIB_DIR / "ADAR1000_AGC.cpp").read_text()
482	        # The constructor initializer list must have enabled(false)
483	        assert re.search(
484	            r'enabled\s*\(\s*false\s*\)', agc_cpp
485	        ), "ADAR1000_AGC constructor must initialize enabled(false)"
486	        assert not re.search(
487	            r'enabled\s*\(\s*true\s*\)', agc_cpp
488	        ), "ADAR1000_AGC constructor must NOT initialize enabled(true)"
489	
490	    def test_mcu_reads_dig6_before_agc_gate(self):
491	        """MCU main loop must read DIG_6 GPIO to sync outerAgc.enabled."""
492	        main_cpp = (cp.MCU_CODE_DIR / "main.cpp").read_text()
493	        # DIG_6 must be read via HAL_GPIO_ReadPin
494	        assert re.search(
495	            r'HAL_GPIO_ReadPin\s*\(\s*FPGA_DIG6', main_cpp,
496	        ), "main.cpp must read DIG_6 GPIO via HAL_GPIO_ReadPin"
497	        # outerAgc.enabled must be assigned from the DIG_6 reading
498	        # (may be indirect via debounce variable like dig6_now)
499	        assert re.search(
500	            r'outerAgc\.enabled\s*=', main_cpp,
501	        ), "main.cpp must assign outerAgc.enabled from DIG_6 state"
502	
503	    def test_boot_invariant_all_layers_agc_off(self):
504	        """
505	        At boot, all three layers must agree: AGC is OFF.
506	        - FPGA: host_agc_enable resets to 0 -> DIG_6 low
507	        - MCU: ADAR1000_AGC.enabled defaults to false
508	        - GUI: reads status word 4 bit[11] = 0 -> reports MANUAL
509	        """
510	        # FPGA
511	        v_defaults = cp.parse_verilog_reset_defaults()
512	        assert v_defaults.get("host_agc_enable") == 0
513	
514	        # MCU
515	        agc_cpp = (cp.MCU_LIB_DIR / "ADAR1000_AGC.cpp").read_text()
516	        assert re.search(r'enabled\s*\(\s*false\s*\)', agc_cpp)
517	
518	        # GUI: status word 4 bit[11] is host_agc_enable, which resets to 0.
519	        # Verify the GUI parses bit[11] of status word 4 as the AGC flag.
520	        gui_py = (cp.GUI_DIR / "radar_protocol.py").read_text()
521	        assert re.search(
522	            r'words\[4\].*>>\s*11|status_words\[4\].*>>\s*11',
523	            gui_py,
524	        ), "GUI must parse AGC status from words[4] bit[11]"
525	
526	    def test_status_word4_agc_bit_matches_dig6_source(self):
527	        """
528	        Status word 4 bit[11] and DIG_6 must both derive from host_agc_enable.
529	        This guarantees the GUI status display can never lie about MCU AGC state.
530	        """
531	        rtl = (cp.FPGA_DIR / "radar_system_top.v").read_text()
532	
533	        # DIG_6 driven by host_agc_enable
534	        assert re.search(
535	            r'assign\s+gpio_dig6\s*=\s*host_agc_enable\s*;', rtl
536	        )
537	
538	        # Status word 4 must contain host_agc_enable (may be named
539	        # status_agc_enable at the USB interface port boundary).
540	        # Also verify the top-level wiring connects them.
541	        usb_ft2232h = (cp.FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface_ft2232h.v").read_text()
542	        usb_ft601 = (cp.FPGA_DIR / "usb_data_interface.v").read_text()
543	
544	        # USB interfaces use the port name status_agc_enable
545	        found_in_ft2232h = "status_agc_enable" in usb_ft2232h
546	        found_in_ft601 = "status_agc_enable" in usb_ft601
547	
548	        assert found_in_ft2232h or found_in_ft601, (
549	            "status_agc_enable must appear in at least one USB interface's "
550	            "status word to guarantee GUI status matches DIG_6"
551	        )
552	
553	        # Verify top-level wiring: status_agc_enable port is connected
554	        # to host_agc_enable (same signal that drives DIG_6)
555	        assert re.search(
556	            r'\.status_agc_enable\s*\(\s*host_agc_enable\s*\)', rtl
557	        ), (
558	            "Top-level must wire .status_agc_enable(host_agc_enable) "
559	            "so status word and DIG_6 derive from the same signal"
560	        )
561	
562	    def test_mcu_dig6_debounce_guards_enable_assignment(self):
563	        """
564	        MCU must apply a 2-frame confirmation debounce before mutating
565	        outerAgc.enabled from DIG_6 reads. A naive assignment straight from
566	        the latest GPIO sample would let a single-cycle glitch flip the AGC
567	        state for one frame — defeating the debounce claim in the PR body.
568	        """
569	        main_cpp = (cp.MCU_CODE_DIR / "main.cpp").read_text()
570	
571	        # (1) Current-frame DIG_6 sample must be captured in a local variable
572	        # so it can be compared against the previous-frame value.
573	        now_match = re.search(
574	            r'(bool|int|uint8_t)\s+(\w*dig6\w*)\s*=\s*[^;]*?'
575	            r'HAL_GPIO_ReadPin\s*\(\s*FPGA_DIG6[^;]*;',
576	            main_cpp,
577	            re.DOTALL,
578	        )
579	        assert now_match, (
580	            "DIG_6 read must be stored in a local variable (e.g. `dig6_now`) "
581	            "so the current sample can be compared against the previous frame"
582	        )
583	        now_var = now_match.group(2)
584	
585	        # (2) Previous-frame state must persist across iterations via static
586	        # storage, and must default to false (matches FPGA boot: AGC off).
587	        prev_match = re.search(
588	            r'static\s+(bool|int|uint8_t)\s+(\w*dig6\w*)\s*=\s*(false|0)\s*;',
589	            main_cpp,
590	        )
591	        assert prev_match, (
592	            "A static previous-frame variable (e.g. "
593	            "`static bool dig6_prev = false;`) must exist, initialized to "
594	            "false so the debounce starts in sync with the FPGA boot default"
595	        )
596	        prev_var = prev_match.group(2)
597	        assert prev_var != now_var, (
598	            f"Current and previous DIG_6 variables must be distinct "
599	            f"(both are '{now_var}')"
600	        )
601	
602	        # (3) outerAgc.enabled assignment must be gated by now == prev.
603	        guarded_assign = re.search(
604	            rf'if\s*\(\s*{now_var}\s*==\s*{prev_var}\s*\)\s*\{{[^}}]*?'
605	            rf'outerAgc\.enabled\s*=\s*{now_var}\s*;',
606	            main_cpp,
607	            re.DOTALL,
608	        )
609	        assert guarded_assign, (
610	            f"`outerAgc.enabled = {now_var};` must be inside "
611	            f"`if ({now_var} == {prev_var}) {{ ... }}` — the confirmation "
612	            "guard that absorbs single-sample GPIO glitches. A naive "
613	            "assignment without this guard reintroduces the glitch bug."
614	        )
615	
616	        # (4) Previous-frame variable must advance each frame.
617	        prev_update = re.search(
618	            rf'{prev_var}\s*=\s*{now_var}\s*;',
619	            main_cpp,
620	        )
621	        assert prev_update, (
622	            f"`{prev_var} = {now_var};` must run each frame so the "
623	            "debounce window slides forward; without it the guard is "
624	            "stuck and enable changes never confirm"
625	        )

665	        assert field_map["doppler_q"].byte_start == 7
666	        assert "detection" in field_map
667	        assert field_map["detection"].byte_start == 9

673	    def test_field_order_and_sizes(self):
674	        """STM32 settings fields must have correct offsets and sizes."""
675	        fields = cp.parse_stm32_settings_fields()
676	        if not fields:
677	            pytest.skip("MCU source not available")
678	
679	        expected = [
680	            ("system_frequency", 0, 8, "double"),
681	            ("chirp_duration_1", 8, 8, "double"),
682	            ("chirp_duration_2", 16, 8, "double"),
683	            ("chirps_per_position", 24, 4, "uint32_t"),
684	            ("freq_min", 28, 8, "double"),
685	            ("freq_max", 36, 8, "double"),
686	            ("prf1", 44, 8, "double"),
687	            ("prf2", 52, 8, "double"),
688	            ("max_distance", 60, 8, "double"),
689	            ("map_size", 68, 8, "double"),
690	        ]
691	
692	        assert len(fields) == len(expected), (
693	            f"Expected {len(expected)} fields, got {len(fields)}"
694	        )
695	
696	        for f, (ename, eoff, esize, etype) in zip(fields, expected, strict=True):
697	            assert f.name == ename, f"Field name: {f.name} != {ename}"
698	            assert f.offset == eoff, f"{f.name}: offset {f.offset} != {eoff}"
699	            assert f.size == esize, f"{f.name}: size {f.size} != {esize}"
700	            assert f.c_type == etype, f"{f.name}: type {f.c_type} != {etype}"

702	    def test_minimum_packet_size(self):
703	        """
704	        RadarSettings.cpp says minimum is 74 bytes but actual payload is:
705	        'SET'(3) + 9*8(doubles) + 4(uint32) + 'END'(3) = 82 bytes.
706	        This test documents the bug.
707	        """
708	        fields = cp.parse_stm32_settings_fields()
709	        if not fields:
710	            pytest.skip("MCU source not available")
711	
712	        # Calculate required payload size
713	        total_field_bytes = sum(f.size for f in fields)
714	        # Add markers: "SET"(3) + "END"(3)
715	        required_size = 3 + total_field_bytes + 3
716	
717	        # Read the actual minimum check from the source
718	        src = (cp.MCU_LIB_DIR / "RadarSettings.cpp").read_text(encoding="latin-1")
719	        import re
720	        m = re.search(r'length\s*<\s*(\d+)', src)
721	        assert m, "Could not find minimum length check in parseFromUSB"
722	        declared_min = int(m.group(1))
723	
724	        assert declared_min == required_size, (
725	            f"BUFFER OVERREAD BUG: parseFromUSB minimum check is {declared_min} "
726	            f"but actual required size is {required_size}. "
727	            f"({total_field_bytes} bytes of fields + 6 bytes of markers). "
728	            f"If exactly {declared_min} bytes are passed, extractDouble() reads "
729	            f"past the buffer at offset {declared_min - 3} (needs 8 bytes, "
730	            f"only {declared_min - 3 - fields[-1].offset} available)."
731	        )
